title: oenothera biennis var. cruciata
intro: Oenothera biennis var. cruciata, also known as Evening Primrose, is a biennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible seeds. The plant contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a polyunsaturated fatty acid with anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Oenothera biennis var. cruciata have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as symptoms of menopause and rheumatoid arthritis. Herbal preparations of Evening Primrose, including oil and capsules, are available and can be used internally or topically to promote overall health and well-being.
